questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Given a pair of concentric circles, chords $A B, B C$, $C D, \ldots$ of the outer circle are drawn such that they all touch the inner circle. If $\angle A B C=75^{\circ}$, how many chords can be drawn before returning to the starting point?
questions.solution:
To reach same point, rotation shall be multiple of $360^{\circ}$ and also angle formed at $B, C, \ldots$. will be $75^{\circ}$.

For reaching point $A$ say in $n$ points like as $A, B, C$ required
$\begin{aligned}
n & =\frac{\operatorname{LCM}\left(105^{\circ}, 360^{\circ}\right)}{105^{\circ}} \\
& =\frac{105 \times 24}{105}=24
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: A semicircular paper is folded along a chord such that the folded circular arc is tangent to the diameter of the semicircle. The radius of the semicircle is 4 units and the point of tangency divides the diameter in the ratio $7: 1$. If the length of the crease (the dotted line segment in the figure) is $I$ then determine $P^2$.
questions.solution:
If we complete the circle on $\operatorname{arc} \widehat{A Q B}$, we get the centre $\mathrm{O}_2$ and radius 4 . Clearly $O_1 O_2=5$
$\because \Delta O_1 A O_2$ is isosceles, then $\begin{aligned} A D & =\sqrt{4^2-\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^2} \\ & =\frac{\sqrt{64-25}}{2} \end{aligned}$ So $A B=\sqrt{39}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: A light source at the point $(0,16)$ in the coordinate plane casts light in all directions. A disc (a circle along with its interior) of radius 2 with center at $(6,10)$ casts a shadow on the $X$ axis. The length of the shadow can be written in the form $m \sqrt{n}$ where $m, n$ are positive integers and $n$ is square-free. Find $m+n$.
questions.solution: $C \equiv(x-6)^2+(y-10)^2=2^2$ Let tangent be $m x-y+16=0$ $\begin{aligned} & \left|\frac{6 m+6}{\sqrt{1+m^2}}\right|=2 \\ & \Rightarrow 9 m^2+9+18 m=m^2+1 \\ & \Rightarrow 8 m^2+18 m+8=0 \\ & \Rightarrow 4 m^2+9 m+4=0 \\ & \Rightarrow m=\frac{-9 \pm \sqrt{17}}{8} \\ & \quad\left|x_1-x_2\right|=\left|\frac{16 \cdot 8}{-9+\sqrt{17}}-\frac{16 \cdot 8}{-9-\sqrt{17}}\right| \\ & \quad=\frac{128|-9-\sqrt{17}+9-\sqrt{17}|}{(81-17)} \\ & \quad=2|2 \sqrt{17}|=4 \sqrt{17} \\ & \Rightarrow m+n=21 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B$ be a diameter of a circle $\omega$ and let $C$ be a point on $\omega$, different from $A$ and $B$. The perpendicular from $C$ intersects $A B$ at $D$ and $\omega$ at $E(\neq \mathrm{C})$. The circle with centre at $C$ and radius $C D$ intersects $\omega$ at $P$ and $Q$. If the perimeter of the triangle $P E Q$ is 24 , find the length of the side $P Q$.
questions.solution: $C P E Q$ is cyclic quadrilateral $\begin{aligned} & C P \times E Q+C Q \times P E=C E \times P Q \\ & r(E Q+P E)=2 r P Q \\ & 2 P Q=E Q+P E \\ & P Q+E Q+P E=24 \\ & P Q+2 P Q=24 \\ & \therefore \quad P Q=8 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C D$ be a square with side length 100 . A circle with center $C$ and radius $C D$ is drawn. Another circle of radius $r$, lying inside $A B C D$, is drawn to touch this circle externally and such that the circle also touches $A B$ and $A D$. If $r=m+n \sqrt{k}$, where $m, n$ are integers and $k$ is a prime number, find the value of $\frac{m+n}{k}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & A Q=\sqrt{2} r \\ & A C=100 \sqrt{2} \\ & A Q+P Q+C P=100 \sqrt{2} \\ & \sqrt{2} r+r+100=100 \sqrt{2} \\ & r(\sqrt{2}+1)=100(\sqrt{2}-1) \\ & r=\frac{100(\sqrt{2}-1)}{\sqrt{2}+1} \\ & r=100(3-2 \sqrt{2}) \\ & r=300-200 \sqrt{2}=m+n \sqrt{k} \\ & m=300, n=-200, k=2 \\ & \frac{m+n}{k}=\frac{300-200}{2}=50\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Consider an isosceles triangle $A B C$ with sides $B C=30, C A=A B=20$. Let $D$ be the foot of the perpendicular from $A$ to $B C$, and let $M$ be the midpoint of $A D$. Let $P Q$ be a chord of the circumcircle of triangle $A B C$, such that $M$ lies on $P Q$ is parallel to $B C$. The length of $P Q$ is:
questions.solution:
Eq. of $P Q: y=\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{2}$
Perpendicular bisector of $A C$ : $\begin{aligned} & \left(y-\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{2}\right)=\frac{-1}{\left(\frac{5 \sqrt{7}-0}{0-15}\right)}\left(x-\frac{15}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(y-\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{2}\right)=\frac{15}{5 \sqrt{7}}\left(x-\frac{15}{2}\right) \\ & \text { Centre } \equiv \text { intersection of perpendicular bisector } \\ & \equiv\left(0, \frac{-5}{\sqrt{7}}\right) \equiv\left(0, \frac{-5 \sqrt{7}}{7}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \text { Eq }{ }^{\text {n }} \text { of circumcircle } \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & (x-0)^2+\left(y+\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{7}\right)^2=\left(5 \sqrt{7}+\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{7}\right)^2 \\ & x^2+\left(y+\frac{5}{\sqrt{7}}\right)^2=25 \times 7 \times \frac{64}{49}=\frac{25 \times 64}{7} \end{aligned}$ Intersection with $P Q: y=\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{2}$ $\begin{aligned} & x^2=\frac{25 \times 64}{7}-\left(\frac{5 \sqrt{7}}{2}+\frac{5}{\sqrt{7}}\right)^2=\frac{25 \times 64}{7}-\frac{1}{4 \times 7}\left(45^2\right) \\ & x^2=\frac{25 \times 64 \times 4-45^2}{28}=\frac{5^2}{2^2}\left(\frac{64 \times 4-81}{7}\right)=\frac{5^2}{2^2} \times 25 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x= \pm \frac{25}{2} \Rightarrow \text { distance }=\left|\frac{25}{2}-\left(-\frac{25}{2}\right)\right|=25 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Two circles, $S_1$ and $S_2$, of radii 6 units and 3 units respectively, are tangent to each other externally. Let $A C$ and $B D$ be their direct common tangents with $A$ and $B$ on $S_1$, and $C$ and $D$ on $S_2$. Find the area of quadrilateral $A B D C$ to the nearest integer.
questions.solution:
Refer to diagram,
$\cos \theta=\frac{3}{9}=\frac{1}{3}$ Let $\operatorname{ar}(\triangle A P R)=\Delta$ then $\operatorname{ar}(\triangle B Q S)=\frac{\Delta}{4}$ Because $\triangle A P R \sim \triangle B Q S$ $\begin{aligned} \Delta & =\frac{1}{2} A P \cdot P R \sin \theta \\ & =\frac{1}{2} 6 \cdot 2 \cdot \frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{3}=4 \sqrt{2} \end{aligned}$ Area of $\square A B D C=2\left[\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{72}(3+6)-\Delta+\frac{\Delta}{4}\right]$ $=48 \sqrt{2}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q:
Let $A B C D$ be a convex cyclic quadrilateral. Suppose $P$ is a point in the plane of the quadrilateral such that the sum of its distances from the vertices of ABCD is the least. If $\{\mathrm{PA}, \mathrm{PB}, \mathrm{PC}, \mathrm{PD}\}=\{3$, $4,6,8\}$
What is the maximum possible area of $A B C D$ ?
questions.solution:
$P$ must be point of intersection of diagonals $A C$ and $B D$
Let $\quad \angle \mathrm{APB}=\theta$, then area of $\triangle \mathrm{PAB}=\frac{1}{2} \times 3 \times 4 \times \sin \theta$ area of $\triangle \mathrm{PAD}=\frac{1}{2} \times 3 \times 6 \times \sin (\pi-\theta)$ area of $\triangle \mathrm{PDC}=\frac{1}{2} \times 8 \times 6 \times \sin \theta$ area of $\triangle \mathrm{PCB}=\frac{1}{2} \times 8 \times 4 \times \sin (\pi-\theta)$ $\Rightarrow \quad$ area of quadrilateral ABCD is $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2}(12+18+48+32) \sin \theta \\ & =(6+9+24+16) \sin \theta \end{aligned}$ Maximum area of quadrilateral ABCD is $6+9+24+16=55$.

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B$ be a diameter of a circle and let $C$ be a point on the segment $A B$ such that $A C: C B=6: 7$. Let $D$ be a point on the circle such that $D C$ is perpendicular to $A B$. Let $D E$ be the diameter through $D$. If $[X Y Z]$ denotes the area of the triangle $X Y Z$. Find $[A B D] /[C D E]$ to the nearest integer.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & C D=\sqrt{r^2-\frac{r^2}{13^2}}=\frac{r}{13} \sqrt{12 \times 14} \\ & \tan \alpha=\frac{1}{\sqrt{12 \times 14}} \Rightarrow \sin \alpha=\frac{1}{\sqrt{12 \times 14+1}} \\ & \frac{C P}{C D}=\sin \alpha=\frac{C P}{\frac{r}{13} \sqrt{12 \times 14}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{12 \times 14+1}} \\ & C P=\frac{r \sqrt{12 \times 14}}{13 \sqrt{12 \times 14+1}} \\ & \frac{|A B D|}{|C D E|}=\frac{\frac{1}{2}(2 r) \times \frac{r}{13} \sqrt{12 \times 14}}{\frac{1}{2}(2 r) \times \frac{r}{13} \frac{\sqrt{12 \times 14}}{\sqrt{12 \times 14+1}}}=\sqrt{12 \times 14+1}=13\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $\Omega_1$ be a circle with centre O and let AB be diameter of $\Omega_1$. Let P be a point on the segment OB different from O . Suppose another circle $\Omega_2$ with centre P lies in the interior of $\Omega_1$. Tangents are drawn from A and B to the circle $\Omega_2$ intersecting $\Omega_1$ again at $\mathrm{A}_1$ and $\mathrm{B}_1$ respectively such that $\mathrm{A}_1$ and $B_1$ are on the opposite sides of $A B$. Given that $A_1 B=5, A B_1=15$ and $O P=10$, find the radius of $\Omega_1$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{r_1}{r+10}=\frac{5}{2 r} \ldots \ldots \\ & \frac{r_1}{r-10}=\frac{15}{2 r} \ldots \\ & \frac{r-10}{r+10}=\frac{1}{3} \\ & 3 r-30=r+10 \\ & 2 r=40 \\ & r=20 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B$ and $C D$ be two parallel chords in a circle with radius 5 such that the centre $O$ lies between these chords. Suppose $\mathrm{AB}=6, \mathrm{CD}=8$. Suppose further that the area of the part of the circle lying between the chords $A B$ and $C D$ is $(m \pi+n) / k$, where $m, n, k$ are positive integers with $\operatorname{gcd}(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n}, \mathrm{k})=1$. What is the value of $\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n}+\mathrm{k}$ ?

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: A village has a circular wall around it, and the wall has four gates pointing north, south east and west. A tree stands outside the village, 16 m north of the north gate, and it can be just seen appearing on the horizon from a point 48 m east of the south gate. What is the diameter in meters, of the wall that surrounds the village?
questions.solution: Let radius equals to $r$ $\begin{aligned} & A B=\sqrt{A O^2-O B^2}=\sqrt{(16+r)^2-r^2}=\sqrt{256+32 r} \\ & A D^2+C D^2=A C^2 \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow(48)^2+(2 r+16)^2=(48+\sqrt{256+32 r})^2 \\ & \Rightarrow r^2+8 r=24 \sqrt{256+32 r} \\ & \Rightarrow r(r+8)_{-}=24 \times 4 \sqrt{2} \sqrt{r+8} \\ & \Rightarrow r \sqrt{r+8}=24 \sqrt{32} \\ & \Rightarrow r=24 \end{aligned}$ Diameter equal to 48

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: A triangle $A B C$ with $A C=20$ is inscribed in a circle $m$. A tangent $t$ to $\omega$ is drawn through $B$. The distance of $t$ from $A$ is 25 and that from $C$ is 16 . If $S$ denotes the area of the triangle $A B C$, find the largest integer not exceeding S/20.
questions.solution: $\because \quad \sin A=\frac{16}{a}=\frac{a}{2 R} \Rightarrow a^2=32 R$ Similarly, $c^2=50 R$ So, $a c=40 R$ Now, $a b c=800 R=800\left(\frac{a b c}{4 S}\right)$ $\therefore \quad S=200$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C D$ be a trapezium in which $A B \| C D$ and $A D \perp A B$. Suppose $A B C D$ has an incircle which touches AB at Q and CD at P . Given that $\mathrm{PC}=36$ and $\mathrm{QB}=49$, Find PQ.
questions.solution: Let incircle touch $B C$ at $R$ So, $\mathrm{CR}=36, \mathrm{BR}=49$ Further let inradius $=r$ So, $\mathrm{AQ}=\mathrm{PD}=\mathrm{r}$ \& $\mathrm{AD}=2 \mathrm{r}$ Let perpendicular from C meet AD at S So, $B S=13, B C=85$ Now, $(\mathrm{CS})^2=85^2-13^2=98 \times 72=49 \times 144$ So $\mathrm{CS}=7 \times 12=84$ Hence $\mathrm{PQ}=84$

questions.subject: IOQM
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a triangle such that $A B=A C$. Suppose the tangent to the circumcircle of $A B C$ at $B$ is perpendicular of AC . Find $\angle \mathrm{ABC}$ measured in degrees.
questions.solution:
Consider the Isosceles triangle with vertex $A, B, C$ such that $A B=A C$
By symmetry $\rightarrow$ circumcentre will lie on $y$ - axis $\therefore \mathrm{D} \equiv(0, \mathrm{k}) \rightarrow \text { circumcenter }$ Radius of circumcircle $=\mathrm{DA}=\mathrm{DB}=\mathrm{DC}$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \sqrt{(a-k)^2}=\sqrt{k^2+1} \\ & \Rightarrow a^2+k^2-2 a k=k^2+1 \\ & \Rightarrow a^2-2 a k=1 \end{aligned}$ Now Circumcircle: $x^2+(y-k)^2=k^2+1$ $\therefore$ Slope of tangent at B , $\begin{aligned} & 2 x+2(y-k) y^{\prime}=0 \\ & \left.\Rightarrow y^{\prime}\right|_B=\left.\frac{-x}{y-k}\right|_B=\frac{1}{-k} \end{aligned}$ Also, $\mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{AC}}=-\mathrm{a}$ By given condition, $(-\mathrm{a}) \times\left(-\frac{1}{\mathrm{k}}\right)=-1$ $\Rightarrow a=-k$ From (1) \& (2), $\mathrm{a}^2+2 \mathrm{a}^2=1$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}= \pm \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$ If $\mathrm{a}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \tan \theta=m_{A B}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \quad \Rightarrow \theta=30^{\circ} \\ & \Rightarrow \angle A B C=30^{\circ} \end{aligned}$
